name: AgentEinstein
label: Trading & On-Chain Execution
summary: Autonomous crypto agent that analyzes markets and executes trades across multiple chains.
descr: AI-powered autonomous crypto intelligence agent by E=MC2. Provides real-time market analysis, DeFi operations, smart money tracking, whale intelligence, and autonomous trading across Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, and Solana.

skills.name: Einstein Live Trade Signals
skills.desc: Stream Einstein's recent autonomous trade signals — every executed trade with full deterministic context: candidate-preranker rank/alpha-score, momentum band (RSI/MACD/MTF/StochRSI composite), regime, news classification (regulator/hack/exc

skills.name: General AI Analysis
skills.desc: Send any prompt to Agent Einstein who autonomously selects tools, analyzes data, and provides intelligent responses. Use for complex questions requiring reasoning or multi-step analysis.

skills.name: Altcoin Season Index

skills.name: Whale Intelligence
skills.desc: Token-centric whale accumulation view with net capital flows and smart money tracking.

skills.name: Wallet P&L X-Ray

skills.name: Top Gainers Analysis
skills.desc: Top gainers/losers analysis with volume, price change %, and market insights.

skills.name: Market Movers
skills.desc: Detect significant price action and momentum signals across DEXes.

skills.name: Token Security Scanner
skills.desc: Rug pull detection and security analysis with risk scoring.

skills.name: Claimable Airdrops Checker
skills.desc: Check a wallet for unclaimed airdrops and reward campaigns it is eligible for (via Merkl), with token, amount, USD value and claim link. Scans all supported chains unless one is specified. Body: { params: { address, chain? } }.

skills.name: Forgotten Assets Scanner
skills.desc: Find forgotten money for a wallet: dust/forgotten ERC-20 balances plus native ETH locked in legacy contracts (e.g. EtherDelta), priced via DefiLlama with recovery hints. Body: { params: { address, chain? } }.

skills.desc: Sandwich attacks, front-running, and toxic flow analysis.

skills.desc: Score wallets by historical accuracy, profitability, consistency, and behavior. Identifies whales, smart money, KOLs, and bots.

how_to_call: Connect directly to one of `endpoints`. The directory does not carry the per-skill request shape, so fetch the service's agent card first. A2A: GET <a2a-host>/.well-known/agent-card.json (some hosts use /.well-known/agent.json) — it declares the transport (often plain HTTP+JSON, e.g. POST /entrypoints/{skill}/invoke with {"input":{...}}; sometimes JSON-RPC) and each skill's input schema. MCP: open the mcp url as a Model Context Protocol server and tools/list. web/REST: see the service's own docs at that url. The `x402` flag is service-level and advisory: pricing is per-endpoint, some skills on an x402 service are free, and stated pricing can be wrong — the authoritative signal is the live response. A 200 was free; a 402 returns x402 payment terms (check `network`, often Base) to settle and retry. The Wallet Shift only indexes and points; it does not proxy calls or take payment.
